title: ELF "Learn to Read" Programme Shows Significant Gains in Lungsen Aspirational Block Schools
content:
560/2025-2026

Lunglei, 26 March 2026:The "Learn to Read" English Literacy Programme, implemented under a collaborative initiative of the Deputy Commissioner's Office Lunglei, Disha Foundation and ELF English, has recorded significant improvement in English literacy among primary school students in Lungsen Aspirational Block of Lunglei District, Mizoram, according to its Annual Report released for March 2026.

Launched in August 2025, the programme covered 10 selected government primary schools and benefitted 268 students. It focused on strengthening foundational English reading skills through a structured phonics-based methodology. The initiative began with an initial assessment to evaluate students' existing proficiency levels, based on which specific learning goals were set for each class.

Teachers underwent online training and were provided with video-based instructional materials via pen drives. Students were given workbooks, while teachers received teaching aids to support effective classroom delivery.

The report highlights that the programme's structured, level-based approach enabled steady student progression, supported by continuous monitoring and tracking of learning outcomes. Classroom activities, reinforced by video lessons, contributed significantly to improved comprehension and learner confidence.

A final assessment conducted in February 2026 evaluated students through both written (offline) and oral (online) formats. The ELF English team, which conducted the oral assessments online, reported marked improvement in students' speaking skills, including better pronunciation and increased confidence.

Comparative analysis between the initial and final assessments revealed substantial learning gains. At the word level, overall student performance improved from 25 percent in the initial assessment to 59 percent in the final assessment—an increase of over 25 percentage points. At the sentence level, Classes 3 and 4 recorded particularly strong progress: Class 3 improved from 18 percent to 66 percent, while Class 4 rose from 13 percent to 74 percent, reflecting more than 50 percentage point gains. Students demonstrated enhanced ability to read, comprehend and interpret sentences.

To encourage participation and recognise achievement, all students were awarded gifts and certificates.

The report concludes that the programme has significantly strengthened foundational English skills among young learners in Lungsen Block, demonstrating the effectiveness of a structured phonics-based approach supported by teacher training and continuous assessment.

Building on this success, the Deputy Commissioner's Office, Lunglei, under the leadership of Deputy Commissioner Pi Navneet Mann, is planning to expand the English Literacy Programme to additional schools in the upcoming academic year 2026–27.

Attachment

title: Lungsen Aspirational Block chhunga school-ahte ELF "Learn to Read" Programme hnuaiah zirlaite'n English bulṭhut thiamnaah hma an sawn
content:
5007/2025-2026

Lunglei, 2026 Vau thla ni 26:Lunglei Deputy Commissioner Office, Disha Foundation leh ELF English-te ṭang ho hma laknaa Lunglei District-a Lungsen Aspirational Block chhunga primary school thlan bika English bulṭhut zirna, ELF "Learn to Read" English Literacy Programme hnuaiah zirlaite'n English thiamna kawngah hma an sawn hle tih March 2026-a Annual Report tihchhuahah tar lan a ni.

August 2025 khan he programme hi ṭan a ni a; sawrkar primary school 10 thlan chhuahah kalpui niin, zirlai 268-in an chhawr a. A ṭan tirhah zirlaite thiamna dinhmun endikna (initial assessment) kalpui hmasak niin, chumi mil chuan pawl tina zir tur ruahman a ni a. Zirtirtute chu online training leh video kal tlangin naupangte zirtir dan tur zirtir hmasak an ni a. Zirlaite chu an tih chhinna tur lehkhabu (workbook) pek an nih bakah, zirtirtute pawh zirtirnaa puitu hmanrua (teaching aids) pek an ni bawk.

February 2026 khan naupangte hmasawn dan endikna, 'final assessment' buatsaih niin, ziak (offline) leh kaa chhang (online)-in zirlaite'n endikna hi an hmachhawn a. ELF English team-te'n online-a zirlaite ṭawngkaa an kawmnaah zirlaite'n ṭawng thiamna, lam rik dan (pronunciation) leh inrin tawkna kawngah hmasawnna nasa tak an nei tih hmuh a ni.

A tir leh a tawpa zirlaite thiamna dinhmun khaikhinin hmasawnna nasa tak hmuh a ni a. Thumal (word level) hriatnaah zirlaite thiamna chu 25% aṭangin 59%-ah a pung a. Thu chang kim (sentence level) thiamnaah Class 3 zirlaite'n 18% aṭangin 66%-ah hmasawnin, Class 4 zirlaite hmasawnna chu 13% aṭangin 74%-ah a kai bawk a. Zirlaite'n English chhiar, hriat thiam leh hrilhfiah kawngah pawh hma an sawn hle a ni.

Zirlaite tihphur nan leh an thawh rah hriatpui nan thilpek leh certificate hlan an ni a. He programme hlawhtlinna avang hian Lunglei District Bawrhsap Pi Navneet Mann kaihhruaina hnuaiah, zirna kum thar 2026–27 lo awm turah sikul tam zawkah he English Literacy Programme hi kalpui chhunzawm tura ruahman a ni.

Attachment

title: Champhaiah Nghakuai Chiah Runpui 2026 hawn a ni
content:
5006/2025-2026

Champhai, dated 26th March 2026:Vawiin chawhma dar 11 khan Keilungliah, Champhaiah Nghakuai Chiah Runpui 2026 (Fish Festival) chu hawn a ni. He hun hi District Fisheries Development Office leh Keilungliah Reservoir Management Committee tangkawpin an buatsaih a ni.

Hawnna hun hi District Fisheries Development Officer Pi Lalremsangpuii'n a kaihruai a, PHE EE Er. Jerome Lalremtluanga'n Keilungliaha hma lak dan hrang hrang leh a chungchang te sawiin hun tha tak an hman a duhsak thu a sawi. Mizoram Angling Association hotute'n thu an sawi bawk a.

Fish Festival hi March ni 28-ah Fisheries Minister Pu Lalthansanga'n a khar ang.

Attachment
